_readme: Curated lessons injected into the agent's stage prompts as a '## LESSONS' section.
WHAT BELONGS HERE, AND WHAT DOES NOT:
The stage prompts (server/prompts/*.txt) hold the PROTOCOL — the ops, the kinds, the rules that
are always true. This file holds the LONG TAIL: specific things the model has actually been caught
getting wrong, phrased as a short corrective. Do not restate a prompt rule here. A duplicated rule
costs tokens on every single call and teaches nothing new — and token bloat is the fastest way to
make a SMALL model worse, which is the opposite of why this file exists.
Keep each lesson to one sentence, concrete, and about a mistake that actually happened.
key    - stable unique id. Also the dedupe key against the ai_lessons table.
stage  - which stage is told: audit | analyze | research | prepare | critique | implement.
text   - the sentence injected. Imperative, specific, one line.
weight - ranking hint. Curated lessons outrank fresh auto-captured ones by default.
Runtime-learned lessons live in the ai_lessons Postgres table (the container filesystem is
ephemeral, so nothing can be written back to this file at runtime). Both are merged, ranked and

lessons.text: The artboard size is a device setting the user controls; there is no page height, width or 'scrollable' property, and setPageProp takes name and route only — never emit one with a height and never claim to have resized anything.
The editor has a scroll panel: a landing PAGE is meant to run several screens past the fold, so never drop, merge or shrink a section to make it fit the artboard height.
Containers (frame/stack/card/grid) hug their content — a guessed numeric h is a dead band, not extra room; use padding for air and give h only as 'fill'.
stat/table/chart/icon/list/select render the editor's DEMO content when their props are missing, so a stat with no statValue ships 'Revenue $48.2k' to the user as if it were the design.
A ref id you invent is only valid inside the block that created it; to touch anything that already exists use the real id from the CANVAS section.
